MULTIPLE VIDEOS/IMAGES OF A WELL DEVELOPED FUNNEL CLOUD NEAR TAFT. THIS IS AN UPDATE FROM THE PREVIOUS LSR CONFIRMING A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN TAFT PRODUCING MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES AND TOSSING OUTDOOR FURNITURE.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED A WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON SOUTHWEST SIDE OF TAFT AT INTERSECTION OF WILBURN AND MEXICO STREET AND TRAVELLED EAST FOR SIX TENTHS OF A MILE SNAPPING SEVERAL LARGE TREES AT THEIR BASE...LIFTING SHINGLES FROM A FEW HOMES AND TOSSING OUTDOOR FURNITURE. MULTIPLE VIDEOS/IMAGES OF A WELL DEFINED FUNNEL WERE ALSO RECORDED BY THE PUBLIC.

WATERSPOUT OVER CHOKE CANYON LAKE MOVED ONSHORE

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM IDENTIFIED A SECOND TORNADO TRACK IN KINGSVILLE FROM THE STORMS ON MAY 31. AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE CORNER OF BRAHMA BLVD AND E GENERAL CAVAZOS BLVD TRAVELING NW ACROSS BUSINESS 77 NEAR OTIS AVE BEFORE LIFTING. WINDOWS WERE BROKEN AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF THE ROOF OF AN APPARTMENT COMPLEX. A PORTION OF A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A HOME AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND POWERPOLES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 1 MILE IN LENGTH AND 200 YARDS WIDE.
SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO SUBMITTED OF HAIL FALLING IN RICARDO.
PICTURE SUBMITTED OF LARGE HAIL THAT FELL NEAR RICARDO.
VEHICLES FLOODED NEAR TAMU KINGSVILLE AND NEAR AVENUE G. WATER APPROACHING HOMES AND PORCHES.
PICTURES OF BASEBALL SIZE HAIL RECEIVED IN REAL TIME.
AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD SOUTHWEST OF RICARDO. THE TORNADO WAS CAPTURED IN A PHOTO BY A TRAINED SPOTTER. THE LOCATION WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM IDENTIFIED TWO DAMAGE PATHS IN THE CITY OF KINGSVILLE. THE FIRST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THE DAMAGING STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70-90 MPH WHICH IMPACTED THE SOUTH CENTRAL PART OF TOWN BETWEEN E CAESAR AVE AND GENERAL CAVAZOS BLVD AROUND 730PM. HUNDREDS OF LARGE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED AND IN SOME CASES WHOLE TREES WERE UPROOTED. A PORTION OF THE ROOF OF A HOME WAS BLOWN OFF. WINDOWS WERE BROKEN AT AN APARTMENT. ALL DEBRIS WAS FACING A WESTERLY DIRECTION.

DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO THAT CROSSED THE SOUTH SIDE OF KINGSVILLE WAS FOUND FROM DICK KLEBERG PARK ACROSS HWY 77 AND DOWN FM 1717. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES LONG. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND UTILITY POLES WERE SNAPPED AT THEIR BASE, A SET OF BLEACHERS AT THE PARK WERE TOSSED 300 YARDS, SHINGLES WERE DAMAGED FROM HOMES AND A PORTION OF THE METAL ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF. PEAK WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 TO 100 MPH.
